There is no information found on the plant Eroteum suberosum, which is likely a misspelling or non-existent plant. However, I can provide information on the plant Erechtites suberosus which is a synonym for Erechtites hieracifolia, also known as burnweed. 

Erechtites hieracifolia is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb native to North America, and its leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties.

The health benefits of Erechtites hieracifolia include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Erechtites hieracifolia include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and inflammatory conditions.
